_comment.1: End-to-end actuation latency test, 2026-07-16 setup: camera
_comment.2: params (56x32, exposure 50, gain 100) and piplate mapping/baud
_comment.3: copied from steering_tuned.json. Steps the x FINE channel
_comment.4: (DAC ch1, 2 V bias, -2 V/unit) as a square wave and measures
_comment.5: serial write -> DAC -> FSM motion -> exposure -> readout/USB ->
_comment.6: centroid. Reports departure and 50% crossing latency plus the
_comment.7: loop rate; full per-edge results append to the results_file in
_comment.9: IMPORTANT: the coarse channels need ~30 s to stabilize after the
_comment.10: json starts, so the piplate start_delay holds the fine channels
_comment.11: at bias for 30 s and warmup extends past it before anything is

params._roi_note: ROI (872,650) matches conf_latency_test.json/conf_bode_*/steering_fsp/attenuation_fsp -- current operating point after the 2026-07-21 realignment (coarse bias 2/2). Harmonized from the stale (832,542) so x and y latency are measured at the identical ROI the loop runs on. Re-derive via contrib/probe_frame.json only on an alignment change.
params._note: 32x32 to match the fsp loop operating ROI exactly (attenuation_fsp.json/steering_fsp.json) so the measured latency is the plant the loop actually runs on. Width does not change the frame rate (height-paced ~3788 Hz), but the readout window should match the loop's. No start_x/start_y: at the coarse biases below the beam parks at the driver-default ROI centre; verify with contrib/probe_frame.json after any bias or alignment change.

pipeline._note: acquire from the brightest pixel for 5 s instead of a hardcoded init, so the test works even if the parked beam is a few px off the ROI centre. Window slightly wider in x (the stepped axis) so the commanded step cannot jump out between frames; height capped at 20 because the frame is only 32 rows.

out_size 2 = pid-style [y, x] vector, matching the piplate index map below. index_cmd 1 drives the x element (DAC ch1), index_err 1 watches CoM x. low/high +-0.5 with scale -2 is a 2 V step around the 2 V bias; raise it if calibration reports step/sigma < 8. warmup 35 s: it must exceed both the CoM acquire_seconds and the 30 s piplate start_delay that lets the coarse channels stabilize. label tells runs apart in the results file; the multi-run driver overwrites it per run. Besides latency, calibration now also reports the plant DC gain (settled CoM step / command step, px per unit minmax command) to the log and results_file -- same quantity/units as bode_plot's fit_K, so each run doubles as a y-gain check.

params.label: yrun5
params._note: y-axis variant: index_cmd 0 drives the y element (DAC ch0, +2 V/unit) and index_err 0 watches CoM y. Everything else matches conf_latency_test.json (the x variant). out_size 2 = [y,x].

pipeline._note: channel map, scales, offsets and baud copied from steering_tuned.json (ch0 = y fine idx 0, ch1 = x fine idx 1, coarse parked at bias with scale 0). start_delay 30 s on the fine channels: the coarse channels need ~30 s to stabilize after startup, and the fine channels must not move until they have. baud 460800 is part of the real feedback path and is included in the measured number.
